title: Prevalence and anatomical distribution of deep infiltrating endometriosis
plain_summary: looked at how common deep endometriosis is and where it tends to show up in the pelvis
relevance: useful baseline for understanding how spread out and hidden the disease can be
abstract_excerpt: Retrospective review of surgical and imaging data from a large patient cohort assessing lesion location and depth.
key_findings: dig lesions were most common in the posterior compartment;imaging under-detected a meaningful share of lesions later confirmed at surgery

title: Endometriosis and the gut microbiome a two way relationship
plain_summary: explores how gut bacteria and endometriosis might influence each other
relevance: relevant to the diet and inflammation angle within endometriosis research
abstract_excerpt: Narrative review summarising animal and early human evidence for bidirectional gut-lesion signalling.
key_findings: dysbiosis may raise circulating estrogen;inflammatory markers were elevated in several small cohorts

title: Endometriosis UK symptom and diagnosis guide
plain_summary: plain language overview of common symptoms and the average time to diagnosis
relevance: good starting point to link laypeople toward before the denser papers

title: Living with endometriosis for 12 years before diagnosis
plain_summary: a personal account of repeated dismissal by doctors before eventual diagnosis
relevance: useful counterweight to the clinical framing showing the human cost of delay

title: Hormonal suppression therapies for endometriosis associated pain a meta analysis
plain_summary: pooled results across trials on hormone based treatments for pain relief
relevance: central reference for the treatment comparison table
abstract_excerpt: Meta-analysis of randomised trials comparing hormonal suppression strategies for pelvic pain outcomes.
key_findings: progestins and combined pills showed similar pain reduction to gnrh agonists with fewer side effects;evidence for long term outcomes remains limited

title: Excision versus ablation surgery outcomes
plain_summary: compares two surgical approaches for removing endometriosis lesions
relevance: relevant if surgical options come up as a discussion point
abstract_excerpt: Cochrane systematic review comparing excision and ablation techniques across multiple trials.
key_findings: excision showed modestly better pain outcomes at 12 months;certainty of evidence rated moderate
